reviews.text: 100 Pierce the veil. Assimilation is a threat to the binding ties of community, while music is a precious, transcendent commodity. This is a film about fighting for what is yours. Keeping it close and keeping it safe and being able to share it with the people you love. A siege horror period drama musical. Drips off the screen with sex and style and warm blood. Generational. More thoughts inpodcastform. 🧛‍♂️ 🎶
reviews.author: SilentDawn

reviews.text: WOW - absolutely loved this. Terrific performances, some truly jaw-dropping music sequences (that first one at the juke joint is an all-timer), and the vampires are just a metaphorical wrapper for a story about racial and cultural identity, appropriation, and legacy. I thought the film was very, very good as the credits started to roll, but the mid-credit sequence elevated it to absolute greatness for me, and fully completed the emotional arc of the whole movie. Felt really bad for…

reviews.text: It’s a horror movie about vampires in the American South, 1932, but that’s all just set dressing. It’s such an intoxicating blend of genres, a movie that is a melting pot just like the cultural heritage this movie explores. A lot of times when one actor plays two characters in a movie, it’s neat but the performances suffer, it feels like a gimmick, but Michael B. Jordan is able make Smoke and Stack feel like two distinct, living, breathing people.…

reviews.text: Coming offEvil Dead Rise, where Lee Cronin proved he could deliver a tight, nasty horror ride, I really expected him to go even harder here. That film was like a 90-minute pressure cooker, brutal, efficient, barely any downtime, just pure nightmare fuel. But this time, it drifts into something way more formulaic. Strip away the extra gore and it’s basically a very polished, very familiar studio horror template. And of course, there is gore, there are some nasty moments,…

reviews.text: there's usually a stereotype among the fans of cinema that no one wants to watch films based on honest protagonists, they rather want them to be grey and dark. Only a few can make this formula of making stories about morality work among people, and that too with so much conviction. Like there's not a even single moment where you want to look away. Hindi cinema has had an amazing year after a while, there are fan favorites like jawan,…

For the first time in my life I hated an intermission, I just wanted to watch the film in one go. Also it's been a while since I've shed a tear in a theatre…

reviews.text: Never have I ever seen any movie in Indian Cinema or world cinema with such a sublime hold on Sound with Cinematography. Yes, I just said a powerful statement, and I just said world cinema, too, and this is all for the combination of Sound and cinematography. This movie needs to be experienced only in one place, which is the theaters; you might not understand how great this film was as a piece of art. Indian films, or I would…

reviews.text: I went in with no expectations and I was certainly surprised. can Indian cinema please create more bangers like this? also the tribute to the iconic music by Ravi Shankar from the Apu Trilogy won me over.

reviews.text: Franz Kafka once said: A belief is like a guillotine, just as heavy, just as light One of the best films of 2023 is not just a film but an inspiration, an epitome of relentless pursuit if you will. It was a tale forged in the impossible as stated by everyone except for the few people he met along the way who believed in him. With the hammer of unyielding effort, he created his destiny. What others deemed as impossible…
reviews.author: Haroon Paul

reviews.text: A man with less exposure comes to Delhi to prepare for the Civil Service examination, and after many heartbreaks and attempts, he manages to crack the UPSC. This is a plotline you all could guess by seeing the trailer or poster of this movie. But where Vidhu Vinod Chopra has broken the cliches is in creating moments in that story. Chopra has taken a very cheesy and predictable drama that shows us the journey of an underdog. Yet, he succeeds…
reviews.author: cinema_made

reviews.text: 'Restart' and 'Haar nahi maanunga' which basically translates to 'I will not give up', I didn't expect these two lines stir me up emotionally and mentally at start of the year. It was beautiful, heartfelt and uplifting experience. It sort of evoked profound emotions inside me after finishing it, That ending had me sobbing ngl. This film serves as a beacon of optimism, dreams, positivity throughout. The presentation of the effort or the whole journey of Manoj Kumar Sharma felt…
reviews.author: Nikhil Das

reviews.text: “Restart!” There are films which genuinelytryto inspire us. And there are films which actuallydoit. Here’s the latest which belongs to this latter bunch. I hate it when others see me teary-eyed in public. But that climax totally wrecked me. I cried. Vikrant Massey, Vidhu Vinod Chopra, and the entire team, take a bow. You all have given us one of the best films of the year. Stop whatever you’re doing and go watch this film in theatre itself. It’s a must-see!
reviews.author: Deepak Chazhoor

reviews.text: "They will kill each other, maim thousands upon thousands, and then hold sacred services and offer up their prayers for having killed so many human beings." Layers of bodies lining the singed world. A spitting inferno of flames, scorched with pools of blood – apart from the roar of flames, and the explosion of land – is the demonic wailing from the ground; the corpses of the dead and undead screaming in a unified terror. This isn't the propheticalHell…

reviews.text: Cold War dick swinging got the United States government to pour all of its money and resources into the Space Race and we put a man on the moon in eight years. Meanwhile, it also got the Soviet government to pour all of its money and resources into a movie and there will never be another one like it. So enormous and grand and ballsy and beautiful and measured and reckless. Landing on the moon is cool and all but have you ever tried CINEMA!?
reviews.author: Chad Hartigan

reviews.text: ***One of the best 150 films I have ever seen.*** ⁍ 135,000,000+ viewers in the Soviet Union between 1966-1968⁍ $100,000,000 budget, which would be surpassed until 1991 withTerminator 2: Judgment Day(1991)⁍ 105,000 tons of kerosene⁍ 12,000 soldiers⁍ 10,000 extras⁍ 10,000 rifles⁍ 7,000 swords⁍ 1,500 horses⁍ 300+ characters with dialogues⁍ 200 firing cannons⁍ 150+ actors for the ballroom sequence⁍ 117 countries released this film in theaters⁍ 103…

reviews.text: I’ll start with my reservations because I think the 5-star rating is fair even in spite of these: The narration and voiceover seem oddly distributed. Some really poetic lines are given in these moments, but it makes you wonder why these lines specifically were lifted from the book and not others, and it at times feels redundant when the image tells us all we need to know (ie Lise’s final scene). While there’s a lot of great experimentation, there were…
reviews.author: Taylor Williams

reviews.text: "Uncertainty, that's what one fears most. " -Napoleon Bonaparte, Those Russians could sure make a moving picture. So, according to letterboxd, this 7 hour film makes it so that I have now watched over 10,000 hours of varying movies and film like projects. This means, as per a defunct theory by Malcolm Gladwell, that I am now officially an "expert" in film. So, in my EXPERT opinion I would like to say that this movie is pretty amazing but I…
reviews.author: {Todd}

reviews.text: Wow! How to write about such a glorious film that clearly spent the Russian financial vaults to achieve artistic glory on the cinematic stage and boy did they. This fires at all pistons in delivering high art. From it's amazing opening shots from above the cloud which also bookend the film to an amazing score and richly rewarding Leo Tolstoy text with his touch of modernity and Natasha as a stand in for Russia moving forward as a nation which…
